summary: While the IT Auditor role perfectly aligns with your desire for predictable morning hours and long-term career sustainability, the cognitive requirements present a significant barrier to your success. The role demands advanced logical deduction and quantitative risk-scoring, which are specific areas where you struggle compared to your preference for protocol-driven work. Ultimately, the mental strain of performing high-level analytical tasks outside of your strength zone would likely negate the wellbeing benefits of the excellent 9-to-5 schedule.
